ai-w12-t1.answer: **RAG** = retrieve relevant docs, then ask LLM to answer using that context. Cuts hallucinations on company-specific knowledge.
ai-w12-t1.realWorld: Employee handbook PDF → chunks → vector search → 'What is leave policy?' answered from real text.

ai-w12-t2.answer: **Embeddings** turn text into number vectors capturing meaning. Similar sentences → vectors close together in space.
ai-w12-t2.realWorld: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small maps 'refund policy' near 'return rules' for search.

ai-w12-t3.answer: **Vector DBs** store embeddings for fast similarity search: pgvector, Pinecone, Chroma. Pick based on ops comfort and scale.
ai-w12-t3.realWorld: pgvector in existing Postgres — no new infra for MVPs.

ai-w12-t4.answer: **Cosine similarity** measures angle between vectors (0–1). Common for text retrieval; higher = more similar.
ai-w12-t4.realWorld: Query embedding compared to all doc chunks — top 5 returned for RAG context.

ai-w12-t5.answer: **Chunking** splits docs into pieces (500–1000 tokens) with overlap. Too big = noise; too small = lost context.
ai-w12-t5.realWorld: Split README by ## headers; overlap 50 tokens so sentences aren't cut mid-thought.

ai-w12-t6.answer: Improve retrieval: better chunks, metadata filters, hybrid keyword+vector, **re-rank** top results with a second model.
ai-w12-t6.realWorld: Filter chunks by product=mobile before vector search — precision up.

ai-w12-t7.answer: **Spring AI** = Spring-native LLM integration: ChatClient, embeddings, vector stores, function calling.
ai-w12-t7.realWorld: spring-ai-openai-starter — beans for ChatModel like other Spring integrations.

ai-w12-t8.answer: **ChatClient** fluent API + **PromptTemplate** with variables. Cleaner than raw HTTP strings.

ai-w12-t10.answer: **Function calling**: LLM requests your Java method (e.g. getOrderStatus). You execute and return result to model.
ai-w12-t10.realWorld: Model says call getWeather(city) — Spring AI invokes @Bean function, feeds JSON back.

ai-w12-t11.answer: **LangChain4j** = Java LLM toolkit: chains, memory, RAG, agents. Alternative to Spring AI, works standalone.

ai-w12-t12.answer: LangChain4j: **EmbeddingStore** + **DocumentLoader** (PDF, URL, file). Ingest pipeline similar to Spring AI.

ai-w12-t13.answer: RAG pipeline: load → split → embed → store → on query: retrieve → augment prompt → generate answer.
ai-w12-t13.realWorld: Eight steps, one Spring @Service class per stage — test each independently.

ai-w12-t14.answer: **Spring AI** if you're all-in Spring Boot. **LangChain4j** for library flexibility or non-Spring apps. Both production-viable.
ai-w12-t14.realWorld: Greenfield Spring Boot → Spring AI first; experiment LangChain4j in spike branch.

ai-w12-t15.answer: **AI agent** plans multi-step tasks: reason → act (tool) → observe → repeat. **ReAct** = Reasoning + Acting pattern.
ai-w12-t15.realWorld: Agent: search docs → call DB tool → draft email — not one-shot prompt.

ai-w12-t16.answer: Production tools: idempotent, timeout, auth, validate args, log invocations. LLM must not bypass security.
ai-w12-t16.realWorld: getAccountBalance(userId) — verify caller owns userId before return.

ai-w12-t17.answer: **MCP** (Model Context Protocol) standardizes how AI clients discover and call tools/resources from servers.
ai-w12-t17.realWorld: IDE agent uses MCP to read repo files and run approved commands — structured, not ad-hoc plugins.

ai-w12-t18.answer: MCP server in Java exposes tools (REST, DB read) via protocol. Clients connect securely with scoped permissions.
ai-w12-t18.realWorld: Internal docs MCP server — dev assistant queries Confluence without copy-paste.

ai-w12-t19.answer: **Streaming** tokens as generated — lower perceived latency. Java: Flux (WebFlux) or InputStream from provider.
ai-w12-t19.realWorld: User sees answer typing live instead of 10s blank wait.

ai-w12-t20.answer: **SSE** (Server-Sent Events) pushes stream from Spring Boot to browser. One-way server→client, simple for chat UIs.
ai-w12-t20.realWorld: @GetMapping(produces TEXT_EVENT_STREAM) — emit token events from ChatClient stream.

ai-w12-t21.answer: Tokens = billed units. Track prompt + completion tokens per request. Cache, shorten prompts, pick cheaper models for simple tasks.
ai-w12-t21.realWorld: gpt-4o for complex; gpt-4o-mini for classification — 10x cost difference.

ai-w12-t22.answer: Model choice: GPT (ecosystem), Claude (long context), Gemini (Google stack), **local** (Ollama) for privacy/dev.
ai-w12-t22.realWorld: Prototype local Llama; prod OpenAI — switch via config profile.

ai-w12-t23.answer: Prod pattern: API gateway → AI service → vector DB + provider. Async for long jobs, sync for chat, queue for batch.
ai-w12-t23.realWorld: Kafka consumer embeds docs overnight; API serves queries from fresh index.

ai-w12-t24.answer: Observability: log latency, tokens, model version, retrieval count. OpenTelemetry traces across HTTP → LLM → DB.
ai-w12-t24.realWorld: Span attributes: ai.model, ai.tokens.input — dashboard cost per feature.

ai-w12-t25.answer: Test AI features: mock ChatModel, assert prompt contains context, WireMock provider, snapshot stable outputs.
ai-w12-t25.realWorld: @MockBean ChatModel returns fixed string — controller test without API cost.

ai-w12-t26.answer: **Evals**: golden Q&A pairs, regression when prompt/model changes. Measure accuracy, not exact string match.
ai-w12-t26.realWorld: 50 FAQ pairs; score % answered correctly after prompt tweak.

ai-w12-t27.answer: CI/CD: run unit tests, eval suite on PR, deploy with feature flag, smoke test /health and sample chat.
ai-w12-t27.realWorld: GitHub Actions: mvn test + eval job blocks merge if accuracy drops 5%.

ai-w12-t28.answer: **Feature flags** roll out AI to 5% users first. Kill switch if cost or quality spikes.
ai-w12-t28.realWorld: LaunchDarkly flag ai_chat_enabled — ops can disable without redeploy.

ai-w12-t29.answer: Microservices: separate **inference service** from core API. Shared embedding service, dedicated chat BFF.
ai-w12-t29.realWorld: Order service doesn't call OpenAI directly — calls internal ai-gateway.

ai-w12-t30.answer: **Kafka** for async AI: document uploaded event → consumer chunks & embeds → index ready event.
ai-w12-t30.realWorld: Decouple upload API from 30s embedding job — better UX with job status.

ai-w12-t31.answer: Gen AI security: prompt injection, output XSS, key leak, data residency. Layer defenses — no single fix.
ai-w12-t31.realWorld: Sanitize HTML in model output before rendering in admin panel.

ai-w12-t32.answer: PII in RAG: redact before embed, filter at retrieval, audit access. GDPR may require deletion from vector store.
ai-w12-t32.realWorld: Don't index customer emails without legal approval — metadata tag internal_only.

ai-w12-t33.answer: Portfolio: RAG chatbot over your docs, streaming API, eval README, cost chart, Docker deploy link.
ai-w12-t33.realWorld: Recruiter asks 'show Gen AI work' — one repo demonstrates full stack.

ai-w12-t34.answer: Interview topics: RAG flow, embedding vs fine-tune, Spring AI beans, token cost, testing mocks, prompt injection defense.
ai-w12-t34.realWorld: Whiteboard: user query → retrieve → prompt → LLM → response in 60 seconds.

ai-w12-t35.answer: Career path: solid Java/Spring → APIs + DB → integrate LLM → RAG prod project → platform/ML engineer teams.
ai-w12-t35.realWorld: Year 1 backend; Year 2 ship internal copilot — realistic progression.
